goal: Make the existing centered wordmark visually read as a logo via color/weight/sizing treatment, without restructuring the .nav-card-top grid (preserves hamburger-top-left mobile UX pattern).
affects: all EN pages: visual treatment of the .nav-brand wordmark in the existing centered grid slot
verified: per-page link tag insertion confirmed via grep across all 7 EN pages
new file contains zero markup changes
grid layout in .nav-card-top intentionally preserved (hamburger stays top-left)
wordmark color, font-weight, and letter-spacing tightened for logo feel
filed: Backlog item Phase 2.1 in CLAUDE.md was the literal text logo top-left links to homepage. Implemented as visual treatment of existing centered wordmark instead of layout restructure (option i over option ii). Hamburger remains top-left on mobile per UX convention. If a real logo graphic is wanted later, it would be a separate task with an SVG asset and a tiny navbar.js edit.

goal: Theme the mobile bottom-nav bar (Features/Today/Draw/Cards/Profile) pastel for visual consistency with the now-light header. Center Draw button intentionally kept gold as the warm accent CTA.
affects: mobile bottom-nav at widths <=768px on all EN pages where bottom-nav is active
verified: body[data-page=home] selector ensures override only fires on homepage during this experiment phase
specificity (0,2,1) ties bottom-nav.css and wins on source order
center button gold preserved by intentionally not overriding .bottom-nav__center-btn
backdrop-filter blur applied for frosted-glass parity with the header
filed: Currently scoped to homepage via body[data-page=home] selector to limit blast radius. If pastel bottom-nav reads well on homepage, the next iteration would unscope to all EN pages (drop the selector prefix in this same file). Scoped rollout pattern mirrors Phase 1 to Phase 2 progression.

goal: Tighten homepage hero typography: bump h1 font-weight from default 400 to 700 (Playfair Display), tighten letter-spacing for elegance, and refine eyebrow pill sizing for stronger visual hierarchy on the homepage chooser screen.
affects: homepage only via existing body[data-page=home] scope
verified: all overrides scoped to body[data-page=home] - zero bleed to other pages
Playfair Display 700 weight available via existing Google Fonts import in HTML
no font-family change - only weight, size, and letter-spacing
filed: Typography refinement extends css/phase-1-home.css rather than creating a new file. Justified because typography changes are tightly coupled to Phase 1 homepage scope and would not extend to other pages independently. If future phases need typography overrides for other pages, those should live in their own scoped files.
